rulings.Case Title: SERVPRO ROHILLCO BUSINESS SERVICES VS. SHARON CASSIDY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 23, 2012, Line 2. DEFENDANT SHARON CASSIDY's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. The motion is denied because the Court does not have a courtesy copy of the moving papers. See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KELLY HOULIHAN VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION STATE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Central Striping Service, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Responses To Admission/Interrogatories (Part 2 0f 2 Of Tentative Ruling).
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 23, 2012, Line 1, Defendant Central Striping Service, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Responses To Admission/Interrogatories (Part 2 0f 2 Of Tentative Ruling). PLAINTIFF'S INITIAL RESPONSES TO THE FORM INTERROGATORIES WERE NOT TIMELY, THUS ALL OBJECTIONS ARE DEEMED WAIVED. PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL WILL PAY TO DEFENDANT CENTRAL STRIPPING SERVICES SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $4,000.00. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT WILL PREPARED AN APPROPRIATE ORDER AND SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON AUGUST 22, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: KELLY HOULIHAN VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION STATE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 23, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT CENTRAL STRIPING SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES (PART 1 0F 2 OF TENTATIVE RULING). Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT CENTRAL STRIPPING SERVICES' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NOSE 5, 6, 7, 8 & 10 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO SERVE NO LATER THAN SEPTEMBER 21, 2012 HIS RESPONSE TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION 5, 6, 7, 8, & 10. IF A RESPONSE TO ANY SUCH REQUEST IS ANYTHING OTHER THAN AN UNQUALIFIED ADMISSION HE WILL PROVIDE WITH SPECIFICITY THE INFORMATION REQUIRED BY FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 17.1 AS TO SUCH REQUEST. *TENTATIVE RULING CONTINUED* =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: HEZZACK DANIELS VS. RECOLOGY INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Recology Inc. (From Case# 508886), Recology San Francisco (From Case# 508886)'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; Compel Responses To Interrogatories, And Request For Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2 Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 23, 2012, Line 2, Defendant Recology Inc. (From Case# 508886), Recology San Francisco (From Case# 508886)'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; Compel Responses To Interrogatories, And Request For Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2 Of Tentative Ruling). ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFFS ARE EACH ORDERED TO SEPARATELY RESPOND TO EACH REQUEST FOR ADMISSION AND, TO THE EXTENT THAT THE RESPONSE IS ANYTHING OTHER AN UNQUALIFIED ADMISSION, HE WILL PROVIDE THE DETAILED INFORMATION REQUESTED BY FORM INTERROGATORY AS TO EACH SUCH DENIAL. SUCH RESPONSES SHALL BE SERVED NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 1 2012. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST THAT SUCH ADMISSIONS BE DEEMED ADMITTED IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS AND THEIR COUNSEL WILL PAY TO DEFENDANT RECOLOGY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,500.00. COUNSEL WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AS TO FORM AND PRESENT TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON AUGUST 22, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: HEZZACK DANIELS VS. RECOLOGY INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; Compel Responses To Interrogatories, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 23, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT RECOLOGY INC. (FROM CASE# 508886), RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO (FROM CASE# 508886)'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; Compel Responses To Interrogatories, And Request For Sanctions (PART 1 OF 2 OF TENTATIVE RULING). Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT RECOLOGY, INC.'S MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION ADMITTED AND TO COMPEL FURTHER INTERROGATORIES RESPONSES IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFFS DISPUTE RECEIPT OF THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, BUT HAVE NOW FILED RESPONSES WHICH PRINCIPALLY CONSIST OF DENIALS AND A RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 WHICH PURPORTS TO APPLY TO EACH SEPARATE DENIAL. THESE RESPONSES DO NOT COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1. *TENTATIVE RULING CONTINUED* =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: NIXON PEABODY LLP VS. GREG SHENKMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 23, 2012, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT NIXON PEABODY LLP's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the common count cause of action is overruled. The cross-complaint sufficiently pleads that claim and plaintiff is raising evidentiary issues that are not properly before the Court at this time. The demurrer to the negligent misrepresentation cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to plead the claim with more particularity. For example, defendant should plead the name of the person making the misrepresentations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE DIAMOND et al VS. YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Independent Medical/Mental Examination Of Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 23, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANTS SERGE RESHKO, VANLENTINA RESHKO'S Motion To Compel Independent Medical/Mental Examination Of PlAINTIFF (PART 1 OF 2 OF TENTATIVE RULING). Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF CHRISTINE DIAMOND'S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS SERE RESHKO AND VALENTINA RESHKO'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER REQUIRING HER TO SUBMIT TO FURTHER MEDICAL EXAMINATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF MAY NOT BE REQUIRED TO SUBMIT TO ANY FURTHER MEDICAL EXAMINATION RELATING TO ANY OF HER PHYSICAL CONDITIONS; HOWEVER, DEFENDANTS MAY HAVE PLAINTIFF EXAMINED BY ONLY ONE QUALIFIED MEDICAL PRACTITIONER LIMITED IN SCOPE TO ANY MENTAL CONDITION WHICH SHE ASSERTS WAS CAUSED BY THE ACCIDENT. NO SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. *TENTATIVE RULING CONTINUED* =302/JPT

Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF'S JAVIER CANDIOTTI'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST TO PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. IT APPEARS THAT THE BASIS FOR THE MOTION WAS THE FAILURE OF DEFENDANT COMMERCIAL MAINTENANCE CORPORATION'S FAILURE TO PROVIDE VERIFICATION FOR ITS RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S FORM INTERROGATORIES - GENERAL SET ONE, FORM INTERROGATORIES - EMPLOYMENT SET ONE AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. DEFENDANT HAS NOW FILED THE VERIFICATIONS, NO EXCUSE HAS BEEN PROVIDED FOR ITS FAILURE TO DO SO IN A TIMELY MANNER.*TENTATIVE RULING CONTINUED* =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: GOUSHIA FAROOK VS. UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA DAVIS MEDICAL CENTER et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Transfer Venue On The Grounds Of Improper Venue And Convenience Of The Witnesses Of Deft'S And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 23, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANTs PRIMO NERY LARA, M.D., REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, JACOB SANDS, M.D.'s motion To Transfer Venue On The Grounds Of Improper Venue And Convenience Of The Witnesses Of Deft'S And Request For Judicial Notice. The motion to change venue is denied. Under CCP 401, Plaintiff properly commenced this action in San Francisco. The application to have the matter transferred based on the convenience of witnesses is denied without prejudice as premature. Such a motion can only be made after the defendants answer. See Easton v. Superior Crt. (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 245; Buran Equipment Co., Inc. v. Sup Crt. (1987) 190 Cal.App.3d 1662, 1665. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
